label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question asks which of the options is wrong according to the news provided. First, I'll go through each option one by one based on the given text. Option (A) says "will give up the space program because of the accident." Looking at the news, the last paragraph mentions President Bush's statement: "America's space program will go on." So clearly, they're not giving up the space program. Therefore, (A) is wrong according to the news. But I need to check all options to be sure. Option (B) states "NASA hasn't found the cause of the Columbia disaster." The second paragraph says, "NASA launched an investigation into the disaster and began searching for the astronauts' remains. It said that although there had been some data failures it was too early to nail down a precise cause." So yes, NASA hasn't confirmed the cause yet, so (B) is correct according to the news, meaning it's not the wrong answer. Option (C) claims "Before the Columbia disaster, no shuttles had exploded in the course of landing." The third paragraph says, "Take-off and re-entry into Earth's atmosphere are the most dangerous parts of a space mission. In 42 years of US' human space flight, there had never been an accident in the descent to Earth or landing. Challenger exploded just after take-off." So the Challenger exploded at take-off, not during landing. Columbia was the first to explode during re-entry/landing. Therefore, (C) is correct according to the news, so not the wrong answer.
